call_transcript:
Agent: Hi Eleanor, this is Mark calling from Finance Solutions. I see you recently inquired about a personal loan on our website. Um, how are you doing today? 
Eleanor: Good, thanks. 
Agent: Great! So, based on your application, we are pre-approving you for a $10,000 Personal Loan at a rate of 8.99%. This could really help with, you know, consolidating debt or maybe a home improvement project? 
Eleanor: I was thinking more about a car. 
Agent: Excellent! A car is a great use of funds. Now, the monthly payments would be approximately $198 for 60 months. Does that sound manageable? 
Eleanor: Yeah, that's… that's pretty good. What are the fees like? 
Agent: There's a one-time origination fee of $200, and that's it. No prepayment penalties. We pride ourselves on transparency. 
Eleanor: Okay. And the interest rate is fixed, right? 
Agent: Absolutely. 8.99% for the life of the loan. 
Eleanor: Alright. Sounds good. Let's do it. 
Agent: Fantastic! I just need to verify a few details…

Agent: Hello Mr. Miller, this is Sarah calling from Finance Solutions. We're offering a special promotion on our Signature Credit Card…
Robert: (Interrupting) Where did you get my number? I told you people yesterday I'm not interested! 
Agent: Sir, I apologize for the inconvenience. We just wanted to inform you about…
Robert: Inconvenience? You're harassing me! I'm on the Do Not Call list, I think! This is ridiculous. I don't need a Signature Credit Card, especially at 19.99% – that's highway robbery! 
Agent: Sir, I understand your frustration…
Robert: No, you don't! Just take me off your list! (hangs up)

Agent: Hi Jessica, this is David from Finance Solutions. Your friend, Michael, referred you to us. He mentioned you might be interested in a personal loan? 
Jessica: Oh, yeah, hi! I am, actually. I'm looking at buying a new car. 
Agent: Wonderful! We can definitely help with that. Based on your credit profile, we are pre-approving you for a $15,000 Personal Loan at a rate of 7.49%. 
Jessica: Wow, that's… that's really good. What's the term length? 
Agent: We offer terms from 36 to 72 months. What are you thinking? 
Jessica: Hmm, probably 60 months. 
Agent: Okay, 60 months it is. Your estimated monthly payment would be around $290. 
Jessica: Okay. And are there any fees? 
Agent: There's a $150 origination fee. But no hidden fees, I promise. 
Jessica: Okay, that sounds reasonable. Can you send me some information about the loan details? I need to think about it a bit more. 
Agent: Absolutely! I can email you a detailed loan agreement right now. Would that be okay? 
Jessica: Yes, please. That would be great.

Agent: Hello David, this is Lisa calling from Finance Solutions. I see you filled out an application for our Signature Credit Card online. 
David: Oh, right. Yeah. 
Agent: Great! We're pre-approving you for a $5,000 Signature Credit Card with a credit limit of $5,000 at an interest rate of 14.99%. It has great rewards points, you know, for travel and cash back. 
David: Hmm. That interest rate seems a little high. I was hoping for something lower. 
Agent: Well, it depends on your credit score, and… 
David: I know, I know. It's just… I'm already getting offers from other companies with better rates. 
Agent: Oh, okay. I understand. We can see if we can adjust it slightly, but it might take a few days. 
David: No, that's alright. I think I'll go with one of the other offers. Thanks anyway. 
Agent: Okay, no problem. If you change your mind, feel free to call us back.

Agent: Hello Mrs. Rodriguez, this is Kevin calling from Finance Solutions. We have a special offer on a Personal Loan…
Patricia: (Interrupting) A loan? I didn't ask for a loan. Where did you even get my number? 
Agent: Ma'am, we are pre-approving you for a $3,000 Personal Loan at 24.99%…
Patricia: 24.99?! What? I… I don't understand. What does that even mean? I'm not good with numbers. 
Agent: It's the interest rate, ma'am. It means…
Patricia: (Interrupting) No, no, I don't want it! Just… just stop calling me! (hangs up)
